fields.label: EE c_id
fields.description: EasyEcom customer identifier — the read-side `c_id` returned by /Wholesale/v2/UserManagement. The join key for this row. Direction-agnostic: the row exists whether the customer was born in EE (pulled in) or in ERPNext (pushed out). UNIQUE at the DB level (§8.2).

fields.label: Status
fields.description: §8.2 review/exception state. Mapped = clean; Created-Flagged = created but with a content problem (e.g. state/pincode mismatch, dirty optional fields); Flagged-Not-Created = unresolvable on the ERPNext side (e.g. India Compliance rejected the GSTIN); Drift = (post-flip) unexpected EE-side change; Disabled = lifecycle-deactivated. Drives the FDE worklist filter.

fields.label: ERPNext DocType
fields.description: Restricted to 'Customer' (§8.2 wholesale population). The form's set_query filters the dropdown; the controller's validate refuses anything else. Dynamic-Link pattern: the doctype controls the options for erpnext_name. (Future entity-sync flows for Supplier / Address would each get their own Map DocType.)

fields.label: ERPNext Name
fields.description: The Customer docname. Dynamic Link resolves the FK target from erpnext_doctype. The pull/push flows write here when they create the corresponding ERPNext Customer. (Linked Billing / Shipping Address records hang off Customer via Frappe's standard Dynamic Link from Address.party_name.)

fields.label: EasyEcom Internal IDs
fields.description: EE's write-side identifier. Per the packet, `c_id` (read) is expected to equal `customerId` (write) — to be confirmed live during Stage 3. Both are stored: ee_c_id is the join key (unique); ee_customer_id is what the push UpdateCustomer call keys on. The packet notes parity is expected but not yet verified.

fields.label: EE customerId
fields.description: EasyEcom internal customerId (returned by CreateCustomer; used by UpdateCustomer). Per the packet's design conversation, this equals ee_c_id — both are stored regardless until parity is confirmed live in Stage 3. Read-only after creation in normal flow; FDE can edit if remapping.

fields.label: Flag Reason
fields.description: For Created-Flagged / Flagged-Not-Created / Drift rows: a plain-English explanation the FDE can act on (e.g. 'state name unresolvable in EE getStates cache', 'state/pincode mismatch (state=Arunachal Pradesh, pincode=560035)', 'India Compliance rejected GSTIN format'). For Drift rows, the structured per-field diff lives in the Drift Fields table below; flag_reason summarises (e.g. '3 fields drifted').

fields.label: Drift Details
fields.description: Populated by the §8.2 post-flip drift detector (Stage 5). One row per field where the incoming EE payload differs from the current ERPNext Customer / Address state. Cleared when the FDE dismisses (status → Mapped) or pushes ERPNext to EE.

fields.label: Drift Fields
fields.description: Per-field divergence. field=ERPNext field name (e.g. 'gstin', 'customer_name', 'billing_address.city'), erpnext_value=current ERPNext value, ee_value=what EE asserts (NOT accepted). Reuses §8d's generic drift-field child DocType (field+value pairs are entity-agnostic).

fields.label: Dismiss Drift (Mark Reviewed)
fields.description: FDE: the EE-side change is wrong / already-handled-upstream. Returns the row to Mapped and clears the Drift table; the underlying ERPNext Customer is NOT touched.

fields.label: Push ERPNext → EE (Re-assert SoT)
fields.description: FDE: ERPNext is the source of truth (per the post-flip contract); push current ERPNext Customer + Addresses to EE via the §8.2 push, overwriting the EE-side divergence.

fields.label: Drift Exclusions
fields.description: Fields the drift detector should IGNORE on this Customer. Reuses §8d's generic exclude-field child DocType. Empty list = compare every field (default Stage-5 behaviour).

fields.label: Push Snapshot
fields.description: Last successful push payload, used to diff against current ERPNext state so the NEXT UpdateCustomer sends only the changed fields. Refreshed automatically on every successful push.
